categories.name: Reactive Globals
categories.description: Injecting reactive variables accessible in all expressions

categories.name: Plugin Security
categories.description: Trusted access, header redaction, and safety checks

entries.name: NoJS.use()
entries.description: Register a plugin with the framework. Accepts a plugin object or a named function.

parameters.plugin: object | function — Plugin object { name, install, init?, dispose? } or named function
parameters.options: object — Optional config passed to install(). Special key: { trusted: true } for unredacted header access

entries.notes: Duplicate names rejected. Function shorthand: named functions only (arrow/anonymous rejected). Cannot install during dispose().

entries.name: Plugin Object Interface
entries.description: The standard plugin object shape with all available properties.
entries.syntax: { name, version?, capabilities?, install, init?, dispose? }

parameters.name: string — Required, unique identifier
parameters.install: function(app, options) — Required, called synchronously by use()
parameters.init: function(app) — Optional, called after NoJS.init() completes (async allowed)
parameters.dispose: function(app) — Optional, called during NoJS.dispose() (async allowed, 3s timeout)

entries.name: Function Shorthand
entries.description: Register a plugin using a named function instead of an object. The function name becomes the plugin name.
entries.notes: Arrow functions and anonymous functions are rejected. The function must have a non-empty, non-'anonymous' name.

entries.name: install(app, options)
entries.description: Called synchronously during NoJS.use(). Register interceptors, globals, directives, filters, validators, events, and stores here.
entries.notes: The app parameter is the NoJS object. Options come from the second argument to NoJS.use(). Must be synchronous.

entries.name: init(app)
entries.description: Called after NoJS.init() completes. DOM is processed, router is active, stores are available. Can be async.
entries.notes: If NoJS.init() already completed when the plugin is installed, init runs immediately (awaited). All init hooks run in installation order. The 'plugins:ready' event is emitted after all init hooks complete.

entries.name: dispose(app)
entries.description: Called during NoJS.dispose() for cleanup. Close connections, clear intervals, flush pending work. Can be async.
entries.notes: Has a 3-second timeout. Plugins are disposed in REVERSE installation order. After all plugins dispose, globals and interceptors are cleared.

entries.name: plugins:ready Event
entries.description: Global event emitted after all plugin init() hooks complete during NoJS.init().

entries.name: NoJS.global()
entries.description: Inject a reactive global variable accessible as $name in all template expressions.

parameters.name: string — Variable name without $ prefix. Must be valid JS identifier. Reserved names blocked.
parameters.value: any — The value to expose. Objects auto-wrapped in reactive context. eval/Function rejected.

entries.example: NoJS.global('theme', { mode: 'dark', accent: 'blue' }); // In HTML:
entries.notes: Reserved names: store, route, router, i18n, refs, form, parent, watch, set, notify, raw, isProxy, listeners, app, config, env, debug, version, plugins, globals, el, event, self, this, super, window, document, toString, valueOf, hasOwnProperty. Prototype pollution keys (__proto__, constructor, prototype) also blocked.

entries.name: Global Reactivity
entries.description: Object values passed to NoJS.global() are automatically wrapped in a reactive context. Mutations trigger DOM updates.
entries.example: NoJS.global('user', { name: 'Guest' }); // updates when $user.name changes
entries.notes: Objects are JSON-round-tripped to strip __proto__ keys, then wrapped in createContext(). Non-serializable objects are deep-checked for dangerous references.

entries.name: Global Ownership Tracking
entries.description: Globals registered during plugin install() are tagged with the plugin name. Overwrites by different plugins trigger a warning.

entries.name: NoJS.CANCEL
entries.description: Read-only Symbol sentinel. Return { [NoJS.CANCEL]: true } from a request interceptor to abort the request.
entries.notes: Throws a DOMException with name 'AbortError'. Handled by the error template if present.

entries.name: NoJS.RESPOND
entries.description: Read-only Symbol sentinel. Return { [NoJS.RESPOND]: data } from a request interceptor to skip the HTTP call and use data directly.
entries.notes: The value becomes the parsed response data, bypassing the network request entirely.

entries.name: NoJS.REPLACE
entries.description: Read-only Symbol sentinel. Return { [NoJS.REPLACE]: data } from a response interceptor to replace the parsed response body.
entries.notes: The value replaces the entire parsed response data.

entries.name: Trusted Interceptors
entries.description: Plugins installed with { trusted: true } receive unredacted HTTP headers and URLs in their interceptors. By default, sensitive headers are stripped.
entries.notes: A console warning is logged when trusted access is granted. Redacted request headers: authorization, x-api-key, x-auth-token, cookie, proxy-authorization, set-cookie, x-csrf-token. Redacted response headers: set-cookie, x-csrf-token, x-auth-token, www-authenticate, proxy-authenticate. Headers matching the pattern x-auth-* or x-api-* are also redacted.

entries.name: Directive Registry Freezing
entries.description: Core directives are frozen after framework load. Plugins can register new directives but cannot override built-in ones.
entries.example:
app.directive('chart', { priority: 25, init(el, name, value) { /* OK */ } });
app.directive('bind', { /* ... */ }); // Warning: cannot override core directive
entries.notes: Frozen directives: state, store, bind, bind-html, model, if, else-if, else, each, foreach, get, post, put, patch, delete, on:*, show, hide, switch, case, class-*, style-*, t, validate, ref, use, drag, drop, animate, route, route-view, computed, watch, call, trigger, error-boundary, i18n-ns, drag-list, drag-multiple.

entries.name: NoJS.dispose()
entries.description: Tear down the entire app: dispose all plugins in reverse order, clear globals, interceptors, and reset init state.
entries.notes: Returns a Promise. Each plugin dispose has a 3-second timeout. After disposal, NoJS.init() can be called again. Calls to NoJS.use() during dispose are ignored with a warning.

entries.name: Prototype Pollution Protection
entries.description: NoJS.global() blocks __proto__, constructor, and prototype as global names. Object values are sanitized to strip prototype keys.
entries.notes: Objects are JSON.parse(JSON.stringify())-ed to remove __proto__ keys. Non-serializable objects are deep-checked for eval/Function references.

entries.name: Dangerous Function Blocking
entries.description: eval and Function references are rejected in NoJS.global() values, even when nested inside objects.
entries.example:
NoJS.global('run', eval);                    // Rejected
NoJS.global('tools', { exec: Function });    // Rejected
NoJS.global('deep', { a: { fn: eval } });    // Rejected
entries.notes: Applies to both direct assignment and deeply nested references.
